heroHeadline: KIA-Led Forces Press on Indaw; EU Extends Myanmar Sanctions; Resistance Captures 68th Junta Position in Karen State; SAC Airstrikes Kill 20+ Displaced Civilians in Thandaunggyi
heroSubtitle: On April 29, 2026 — Day 1,913 of Myanmar's post-coup civil war — KIA-led and PDF-affiliated forces are pressing toward Indaw town on the Katha-Sagaing axis, with a junta column reaching 6 miles from the town; Myanmar Now reported resistance capture of Indaw after months of fighting. In Kachin State, KIA Brigade 1 ambushed a 70-man SAC column at Wadat Village near Putao, inflicting heavy casualties in the latest multi-axis KIA offensive. On April 28, the EU extended Myanmar sanctions until April 30, 2027 (105 individuals, 22 entities), underscoring that major democracies reject the junta's 'civilian rule' transition despite China's diplomatic backing. PDF and Pa Ka Pha forces captured the Aung Chan Thar base in Kalewa Township (Sagaing), reopening the Ye-U–Kalewa road. The KNLA confirmed the capture of Kabawtu base in Hpapun District — the 68th junta position seized in Mutraw since 2021. On April 27, SAC airstrikes killed 20+ displaced civilians (IDPs) in Thandaunggyi Township (Ngwe Taung, Bahan, Taw Pone Auk villages), with a combined Day 1,911 death toll of ~30 across Karen and Tanintharyi. The SCEF unified resistance council (NUG, CRPH, KIO, KNU, KNPP, CNF) launched on April 27 remains the most institutionally significant anti-junta coordination structure since the 2021 coup.
footerNote: Data sourced from AAPP, ACLED, UN OCHA, Irrawaddy, RFA, Myanmar Now, DVB, Al Jazeera, Mizzima, Human Rights Watch, Crisis Group, Myanmar Peace Monitor, MoeMaKa CDM News, Japan Times, Khaosod English, The Nation Thailand, Foreign Policy, Xinhua, CGTN, SCMP, Free Malaysia Today, BERNAMA, Anadolu Agency, IMNA/Mon News, FDD, China MFA, Shan Herald Agency for News, The Diplomat, EU Council, and official NUG, KNU, KIA, AA, and PDF statements.
